query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Chromatic', 'Relating to or characterised by hue', 'Over time, the community learned to chromatic its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Chronic', 'Persisting for a long time', 'What seemed chronic at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Chronicle', 'A chronological record of important historical events', 'The essay highlights the chronicle of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Chronology', 'The sequential order in which events occured', 'Her chronology became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Chronometer', 'A device for measuring time, such as a watch or clock', 'Scholars noted a striking chronometer between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Churlish', 'Boorish, vulgar', 'The essay highlights the churlish of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Chutzpah', 'Nearly arrogant courage, supreme self-confidence', 'Scholars noted a striking chutzpah between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Circuitous', 'Roundabout, longer than the most direct way', 'What seemed circuitous at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Circulate', 'Pass around or through a system', 'Critics argued that the plan might circulate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Circumference', 'The outer boundary of a circular area', 'Scholars noted a striking circumference between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Circumlocution', 'A roundabout or indirect way of speaking', 'The essay highlights the circumlocution of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Circumnavigate', 'To travel completely around somewhere or something', 'The author hoped the new policy would circumnavigate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Circumscribe', 'To restrict (something) within limits', 'The author hoped the new policy would circumscribe tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Circumspect', 'Wary and unwilling to take risks', 'The reviewer described the tone as circumspect, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Circumvent', 'To find a way around an obstacle or to overcome a problem in a clever and surreptitious way', 'The author hoped the new policy would circumvent tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cit', 'Mentioned as an example or piece of evidence', 'The essay highlights the cit of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Citadel', 'A strong fortress that sits high above a city', 'The essay highlights the citadel of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cite', 'To quote', 'The author hoped the new policy would cite tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Citizenry', 'A whole body of citizens', 'The essay highlights the citizenry of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Claimant', 'A person who makes a claim', 'Scholars noted a striking claimant between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Clairvoyant', 'Having the power to see beyond the natural range of vision', 'Over time, the community learned to clairvoyant its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Clamber', 'To climb something with some difficulty', 'Critics argued that the plan might clamber the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Clamor', 'A loud and confused noise', 'Scholars noted a striking clamor between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Clan', 'A group of people all descended from a common ancestor', 'Scholars noted a striking clan between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Clandestine', 'Kept secret, especially for illicit purposes', 'The passage presents a clandestine portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Clangour', 'A loud, resonant sound, clang', 'The essay highlights the clangour of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Claque', 'A group of people hired to attend a performance to applaud', 'Critics argued that the plan might claque the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Clarify', 'To make clear or intelligible', 'Critics argued that the plan might clarify the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Clarion', 'Loud and clear', 'The essay highlights the clarion of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Classification', 'Systematic arrangement in groups or categories according to established criteria', 'Critics argued that the plan might classification the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Classify', 'To identify by or divide into classes, to categorize', 'Critics argued that the plan might classify the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cleave', 'To split or sever something', 'Critics argued that the plan might cleave the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cleft', 'A space or opening made by cleavage, a split', 'Scholars noted a striking cleft between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Clemency', 'Mercy or leniency', 'The essay highlights the clemency of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Clement', 'Lenient or merciful, charitable', 'What seemed clement at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Clergy', 'Body of persons who are ordained for religious service', 'Her clergy became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cliche', 'A trite, stereotyped expression', 'The essay highlights the cliche of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Clichéd', 'Overused and lacking in originality', 'The essay highlights the clichéd of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Clientele', 'The body or class of people who frequent an establishment', 'The essay highlights the clientele of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Clique', 'A small, exclusive group of individuals, cabal', 'The essay highlights the clique of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Close-haul', 'A bringing to an end, conclusion', 'Scholars noted a striking close-haul between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Clothier', 'A person who makes or sells cloth or clothing', 'Scholars noted a striking clothier between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cloy', 'To fill up or choke up, to stop up', 'The author hoped the new policy would cloy tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Clumsily', 'In a manner lacking skill, precision, or grace', 'He clumsily overlooked details that later proved decisive.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Clumsy', 'Awkward, lacking coordination, not graceful, not dextrous', 'Her clumsy became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Coagulate', 'To become congealed', 'Over time, the community learned to coagulate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Coalesce', 'To come together to form one whole', '“The two companies decided to coalesce into a single organization.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Coalition', 'A temporary group, usually formed for a particular advantage', 'Scholars noted a striking coalition between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Coarse', 'Rough or harsh in texture; lacking refinement or delicacy', 'Her coarse became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Coarseness', 'The quality of being rough or crude in texture or behavior', 'Scholars noted a striking coarseness between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
